commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: Doctor: structure gateway service repairs
target.body:
## Summary

This PR moves the gateway services doctor pass onto the structured repair runner while keeping the legacy lint/read-only presentation intact.

- Splits gateway service detection from repair so doctor --fix can report dry-run changes, diffs, and service effects through the shared repair runner.
- Keeps the public health contract unchanged; these are core doctor internals only.
- Preserves gateway service audit behavior, including planned managed env keys, expected service PATH, install-plan warnings, token drift warnings, source-checkout warnings, and running-service rewrite blocking.
- Keeps platform notes positional and note-based so user-visible lint behavior remains the same.

## Verification

- pnpm install
- git diff --check
- oxfmt check on touched doctor files
- oxlint on touched doctor files
- tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot (4 files, 74 tests)
- codex review --uncommitted accepted one expectedServicePath regression, fixed it, reran proof, then reran review clean: no discrete correctness issues

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: doctor --fix can now route gateway service cleanup/config repair through structured dry-run/diff/effects while lint/read-only gateway service notes remain positional.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 OpenClaw worktree with local package install.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot

Evidence after fix: focused tests include gateway service structured detection, planned install warnings, expected service PATH audit input, positional gateway repair runner routing, dry-run/diff option forwarding, and legacy platform-note preservation.

Observed result after fix: 4 test files passed, 74 tests passed; format, lint, core typecheck, core test typecheck, and Codex review are clean.

What was not tested: no live launchd/systemd service was installed or removed in this local proof; those paths remain covered by mocked gateway service tests in this slice.

commands.author: jason-allen-oneal

target.title: fix(codex): mirror accepted prompts early
target.body:
## Summary
- Mirror the accepted Codex app-server user prompt as soon as the turn has a stable id.
- Use the context session key for the early transcript mirror so WebChat/Control UI session attribution is not replaced by the sandbox session key.
- Reuse the existing `turnId:prompt` mirror identity and thread-scoped idempotency so the final transcript mirror dedupes the prompt.
- Add regression coverage for split `sessionKey` and `sandboxSessionKey` behavior.

Fixes #83528

## Proof
- Head: `ef0169f0d7308f9f496303afbb2584e8d86b60c2`.
- PR diff contains only:
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts`
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- Formatting: `git diff --check` passed.
- Targeted regression passed:
  - `CI=1 NO_COLOR=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run --reporter=dot --testNamePattern "mirrors the accepted prompt before Codex turn completion using the context session key"`
- The previous failing check was the PR-body proof parser; this update uses the required field labels from `docs/ci.md`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: The Codex app-server run attempt mirrors the accepted user prompt into the OpenClaw session transcript as soon as the turn has a stable id, before Codex turn completion. When `sessionKey` and `sandboxSessionKey` differ, the early mirror uses the context session key for transcript write hooks and transcript update attribution. The final transcript mirror still dedupes the same user prompt through the existing `turnId:prompt` identity and thread-scoped idempotency key.

- Real environment tested: Clean isolated OpenClaw worktree at PR head `ef0169f0d730` on Linux, using the bundled Codex app-server run-attempt implementation and OpenClaw's session transcript writer/hook path.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From `/home/rev/projects/openclaw-pr-84652-clean`, verified the diff, ran whitespace validation, ran the focused Codex app-server prompt-mirroring runtime regression, and locally evaluated this PR body with `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs` before publishing it.

- Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the patched worktree:

  ```text
  $ git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts

  $ git diff --check
  # no output

  $ CI=1 NO_COLOR=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run --reporter=dot --testNamePattern "mirrors the accepted prompt before Codex turn completion using the context session key"
  RUN  v4.1.7 /home/rev/projects/openclaw-pr-84652-clean

  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  1 passed | 215 skipped (216)
    Duration  11.77s (transform 8.66s, setup 409ms, import 11.04s, tests 178ms, environment 0ms)

  $ node --input-type=module -e 'import { evaluateRealBehaviorProof } from "./scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs"; /* evaluated updated PR body */'
  External PR includes after-fix real behavior proof.
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The accepted user prompt was written to the transcript before Codex turn completion. The split session-key regression verified the early write was attributed to `agent:webchat:session-1`, not `agent:sandbox:session-1`. After final mirroring, the transcript still contained only one user row for that prompt, proving the early and final transcript mirrors dedupe through the same `turnId:prompt` identity.

- What was not tested: Did not run a full live Codex app-server turn against a real model provider. Did not capture WebChat/Control UI visual proof. Did not test concurrent prompt mirroring from multiple Codex threads.
